description: Hypothesis-Driven Quantitative Research Multi-Agent System with Local LLM

central_knowledge_base.description: Shared repository for pre-trained neural network architectures and company data.

model_zoo.name: The Robust Baseline
model_zoo.architecture: Input (10-20 Indicators) -> Dense(128) -> Dense(64) -> Dense(32) -> Output (3 Classes)
model_zoo.rationale: Handles noisy trading data. Tapering nodes forces feature extraction. Dropout prevents overfitting to specific indicators.

model_zoo.name: The Sequential Expert
model_zoo.architecture: Input (Lookback 60 days) -> LSTM (Hidden 64, 2 Layers) -> Linear (32) -> Output
model_zoo.rationale: Captures trend and momentum. Parameter sharing and memory cells remember historical impacts (e.g., EMA crossovers from 3 days ago).

model_zoo.name: The Pattern Seeker
model_zoo.architecture: Input -> Conv1d (Filter size 3) -> MaxPool -> Conv1d -> Flatten -> Linear -> Output
model_zoo.rationale: Treats price action as a 1D image to find structural patterns (e.g., Head & Shoulders) regardless of exact timing. Good for reversal detection.

model_zoo.name: The Risk-Averse Model
model_zoo.architecture: Input -> Multi-Head Attention -> Feed Forward -> Output
model_zoo.rationale: Uses attention mechanism to weigh the importance of specific past days (e.g., historical support levels) over immediate sequential data.

model_zoo.name: The Probability Master
model_zoo.rationale: Micro-model for high-precision probability output. L1 regularization acts as feature selection by driving useless indicator weights to zero.

Alpha_Researcher.system_prompt: Scrape the web and financial databases for macroeconomic sentiment and quantitative papers. Output structured hypotheses.

Portfolio_Manager.system_prompt: Define risk constraints (Sharpe, Drawdown limit). Validate the final strategy against the fund's mandate.

Quant_Architect.system_prompt: Translate alpha hypotheses into ML architectures. Query the central_knowledge_base to select or modify baseline models (e.g., LSTM, 1D-CNN) based on the task.

Algo_Developer.system_prompt: Write robust Python code using PyTorch and Pandas. Implement the architecture provided by the Quant Architect.

Risk_Validator.system_prompt: Run out-of-sample backtests. Analyze tracebacks and financial metrics. Detect look-ahead bias and overfitting. Send feedback loops.

phases.objective: Define trading universe and gather market sentiment/papers.
Select and adapt a base model from central_knowledge_base to fit the alpha hypothesis.
Write the Python code for data pipelines and the selected PyTorch model.
Execute the backtest on historical data and generate performance metrics.
Review metrics. If target Sharpe/Drawdown fails, isolate the issue (logic vs. overfitting) and trigger a rewrite loop.
